creator: ino-qc@spotteswoode.de.eu.org
text:
> Frank J. Laszlo:

> Could you please check the version of
> ports/devel/rlwrap/Makefile? This problem was fixed on 12/12/07. Are
> you using devfs? Cheers.

no, i do not use devfs.

> (11:03:03) [laszlof@main /usr/ports/devel/rlwrap]% ident Makefile
> Makefile:
>      $FreeBSD: ports/devel/rlwrap/Makefile,v 1.23 2006/12/12 12:06:37
> laszlof Exp $

'ident /ports/devel/rlwrap/Makefile'
/ports/devel/rlwrap/Makefile:
     $FreeBSD: ports/devel/rlwrap/Makefile,v 1.23 2006/12/12 12:06:37 laszlof Exp $

i had just found what i thought was a workaround:  "make patch;cd
work/rl*;./configure --enable-debug;gmake;gmake install".  this produced
a working binary.  after reading your email, i double-checked by doing
"make clean;make {d,r}einstall", but this time, everything worked fine.

since i didn't cvsup after the mourning of this day, i cannot explain
this.  ports/devel/rlwrap could have been a port where i forgot a "make
clean" in the distant past and again before installing it anew today.
but then the installation step would have informed me about the existing
install.

so i cannot explain why the port failed earlier, and in a way you
obviously recognized, it works now as advertized.

sorry for the inconvenience and all the aggravation,


regards, clemens
